feat(terraform): make platform MI group membership conditional

Add platform_mi_group_member_enabled variable (default: true) so the
azuread_group_member resource can be disabled if the bootstrap SP lacks
Group.ReadWrite.All permission on Microsoft Graph.

Requires SP to have Group.ReadWrite.All (Application, admin-consented)
to add a Managed Identity as a group member. GroupMember.ReadWrite.All
alone is insufficient — Group.ReadWrite.All must also be granted.

commit.message:
feat: add FPS counter to verify 300-particle performance

Adds a live FPS display (#fps-display) in the controls panel.
- Shows frameRate() rounded + current particle count
- Updated every 30 frames to be readable without flicker
- Colour-coded: green >=30fps (target), yellow 20-29, red <20

At 300 particles the O(n^2) engine makes ~44,850 dSq checks; roughly
~5,000 pairs pass the MAX_RANGE gate and compute actual forces.
Modern browsers handle this well above the 30fps target; the counter
makes this directly observable without needing dev tools.

fix: ghost rendering at canvas edges for true torus look (closes #9.2)

Particles near a canvas edge were teleporting to the other side, breaking
the toroidal-space illusion. Fix: render ghost copies at the wrapped position
whenever a particle is within RENDER_RADIUS (14 px) of any edge.

Implementation:
- drawParticleAt(p, x, y): core rendering at arbitrary position
- drawParticle(p): builds xs/ys arrays, cross-product ensures all 4 edges
  and all 4 corners are handled correctly (up to 4 draws per particle)
- RENDER_RADIUS = 14 derived from outer glow diameter (28px / 2)

Bond lines across the boundary remain skipped; that is the next step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: pre-publish LLM-agnostic verification

- pkg/registry/verify: new package - verify.Dir() walks all YAML files
  in a package directory and checks for policy violations:
  ERROR: hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, bot tokens,

* Initial plan

* security: fix SEC-001/004/005/008/013 vulnerabilities

SEC-001: Overseer terminal XSS via game:event data
- Overseer.print() and addMessage() used div.innerHTML = String(text)
  without escaping, allowing injected HTML in poi names, inventory items,
  alert messages, and caps balance to execute as script
- Added _overseerEscapeHtml() helper; all terminal print paths now escape
  before insertion

SEC-004: fizz-fun /admin/launch IDOR (wallet from req.body)
- Endpoint had no authMiddleware; admin check read wallet from attacker-
  controlled req.body allowing anyone to test any wallet against ADMIN_WALLETS
- Added authMiddleware; wallet now sourced from verified session (req.player)

SEC-005: Player name has no length or character restriction
- POST /api/player/create accepted arbitrary strings including HTML/script tags
  with no length cap, enabling stored XSS via leaderboard/terminal display
- Name is now stripped of HTML-special chars and limited to 32 characters

SEC-008: fizz-fun endpoints have no rate limiting
- All seven fizz-fun routes had zero rate limiting, enabling enumeration,
  Solana RPC quota drain, and abuse of bonding-curve math endpoints
- Added fizzReadLimiter (60 req/min) and fizzWriteLimiter (10 req/min)

SEC-009: fizz-fun leaks raw error messages to clients
- Replaced res.status(500).json({ error: err.message }) with generic
  'Internal server error' in all fizz-fun 500 handlers

SEC-013: /api/npc-context/:npcId/encounter unauthenticated, no rate limit
- Endpoint called xAI Grok / HF API with no auth and no per-route limiter;
  any unauthenticated caller could drain API token quota
- Added authMiddleware + encounterLimiter (5 req/min)
- Added npcContextLimiter (20 req/min) to static NPC context routes

.gitignore: added .env.testnet to prevent future accidental commits of
environment-template files with default credentials

* security: address code review feedback on SEC-001/004/005/013 fixes

- overseer addMessage: simplify comment to match always-escape implementation
- player name: use allowlist regex [^a-zA-Z0-9\s\-_.] instead of denylist
  to block homograph/injection vectors and disallow emoji/unicode abuse
- npc-context encounterLimiter: use wallet-based key generator so shared IPs
  (NAT/corporate proxies) don't exhaust quota for all users behind the same IP
- fizz-fun admin/launch: log warning when ADMIN_WALLETS env var is empty to
  catch deployment misconfiguration; add fizzWriteLimiter to the route

fix: prevent particle freeze — BETA repulsion + overlap impulse + thermal noise (closes #9.1)

Root cause: attracting particles collapsed to d=0 (exact-overlap force skip +
DAMPING=0.98 killed residual velocity), leaving frozen dimers throughout the sim.

Changes:
- d===0: now emits random separation impulse (MIN_DIST magnitude) instead of
  silently skipping, breaking frozen exact-overlap states
- BETA=10 / BETA_REPULSE=120: universal short-range repulsion for d<BETA prevents
  all pairs from collapsing; force ramps linearly from -BETA_REPULSE at d=0 to 0
  at d=BETA (smooth join with outer type-specific force)
- DAMPING 0.98 → 0.99: slower velocity decay gives Fluxar/Phasex perturbations
  more time to keep the system in motion
- Tiny thermal kicks (±0.02 px/frame) in draw() after updateParticles prevent
  complete stillness in symmetric many-body equilibria (Brownian motion analogue)
- Unit test 4.2 updated: replaced MIN_DIST-clamp equality assertions with
  BETA-repulsion gradient tests (d=2 more repulsive than d=5; at d=BETA only
  type-specific attraction remains)

fix: botão disponibilizar em bloco não deve habilitar sem mapa criado

Situações MAPEAMENTO_CADASTRO_HOMOLOGADO e REVISAO_CADASTRO_HOMOLOGADA
eram incluídas erroneamente como elegíveis em podeDisponibilizarEmBloco.
Ambas são anteriores à criação do mapa de competências, o que causava o
botão habilitado indevidamente e falha com sucesso+erro simultâneos ao
confirmar.

Conforme CDU-24 e o fluxo de estados (_intro.md), apenas MAPEAMENTO_MAPA_CRIADO
e REVISAO_MAPA_AJUSTADO habilitam o botão.

Closes #1520

feat: add FORCE_SCALE=400 to make simulation dynamics lively (task 8.2)

Without a force multiplier the raw matrix strengths (max 1.5) produce
F_max = 1.5 / (5²) = 0.06 — far below BOND_THRESHOLD (0.5) and too
weak to drive particles toward their maxSpeed at typical distances.

Added FORCE_SCALE = 400 applied uniformly in applyForces:
  F = strength * FORCE_SCALE / (d_clamped²)

Result:
- A single Lumion-Lumion pair at d=100 now generates a terminal
  velocity of ~1.6 px/frame; multiple neighbors combine to reach maxSpeed.
- Bonds form when strong pairs are within ~35-55 px (e.g. Nullon-Gravon
  threshold distance = sqrt(1.5*400/0.5) ≈ 35 px).
- PHASE and CHAOS interactions now produce visible oscillating/chaotic
  motion rather than noise too small to see.

Also updated 4.5 unit tests:
- Removed the stale 'bonds can never form' comment block.
- Added explicit no-bond case (Lumion-Lumion at d=100, F=0.032 < 0.5).
- Added explicit bond case (Nullon-Gravon at d=5, F=24 >> 0.5).
- Updated invariant check to include FORCE_SCALE in the F formula.
- Fixed stale 4.2 comment that hardcoded the old F value.

commit.message:
Implement arena-based region deallocation (Phase 1) (#65)

* Implement arena-based region deallocation (Phase 1)

Add SaveArenaState/RestoreArenaState IR instructions that snapshot and
restore the heap bump-allocator cursor at ownership scope boundaries.

When a scope's result is a copy type (Int, Float, Bool), RestoreArenaState
resets the heap cursor to the scope-entry watermark after Drop instructions
have run, effectively freeing all heap memory allocated within the scope.

Changes:
- Ir.cs: Add SaveArenaState and RestoreArenaState IR instruction records
- Lowering.cs: Emit SaveArenaState at PushOwnershipScope, conditionally
  emit RestoreArenaState at PopOwnershipScope for copy-type results
- LlvmCodegen.cs: Dispatch new instructions to backend codegen
- LlvmCodegenMemory.cs: Implement EmitSaveArenaState/EmitRestoreArenaState
- ArenaDeallocationTests.cs: 16 new tests covering IR emission, slot
  pairing, copy vs heap type behavior, nesting, and Drop interaction
- Update snapshot verified files for new IR shape
- Update COMPILER_OPTIMIZATION.md to document Phase 1 completion

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix arena watermark placement: emit SaveArenaState before let-bound value lowering

Decouples SaveArenaState emission from PushOwnershipScope into a separate
EmitArenaWatermark() method. In LowerLet, the watermark is now emitted
BEFORE lowering let.Value so heap allocations from the bound expression
(lists, tuples, ADTs) are covered by the arena scope.

Adds 3 tests verifying SaveArenaState precedes heap Alloc instructions
for list and tuple let-bound values, and that copy-type bodies still
emit RestoreArenaState to reclaim those allocations.

Add Chapter 13: Object Graph Validation

Inventory how each validation invocation model handles complex object
graphs with recursive walks and how validation results are represented:

- Core Validator.TryValidateObject: single-level only, no recursion
- ASP.NET Core MVC: full recursive graph walk via ValidationVisitor
  with cycle detection and configurable MaxValidationDepth
- Blazor: top-level only by default; experimental ObjectGraphDataAnnotationsValidator
- Options validation: opt-in recursion via [ValidateObjectMembers]
  and [ValidateEnumeratedItems] attributes
- Microsoft.Extensions.Validation (.NET 10): full recursive graph walk
- EF Core and OpenAPI: metadata only, no runtime validation

Ecosystem projects that fill the recursion gap:
- MiniValidation (Damian Edwards): recursive with cycle detection
- RecursiveDataAnnotationsValidation, DataAnnotationsValidatorRecursive
- FluentValidation: child validators and RuleForEach

Includes comparison table and implications for async validation design.
Updates nav links on ch12, appendix-a, and README TOC.
